This article nails the exact problem space. "AI amnesia" isn't a model failure — it's a **systems engineering failure** in the context pipeline.

https://hackernoon.com/ai-memory-should-be-product-state-not-a-hidden-prompt-trick

This article hits the exact right note: **memory as governed handoff artifacts, not hidden prompt stuffing**.

## Core Thesis

> "If memory changes future product behavior, it cannot stay a hidden prompt trick."

The author reframes memory from "what can the model remember?" to:
- What should be carried forward?
- Who approved it?
- When can it enter the next prompt?
- When should the system deliberately forget it?

## Architecture Match to OpenClaw

https://hackernoon.com/modal-logic-and-neural-networks

This article makes a fascinating philosophical argument: **modal logic as an interpretive framework for neural networks**, shifting from "how tensors change" to "what meaning persists."

## Core Thesis

> "Rather than asking how tensors change numerically, modal logic asks what information remains necessarily true as those tensors change."

The author proposes viewing neural network operations through **modal logic** (logic of necessity and possibility) instead of pure linear algebra:
